docs.title: About SAPAA
docs.text:
The Stewards of Alberta's Protected Areas Association (SAPAA) applications help stewards, coordinators, and administrators monitor and track site inspections across Alberta's protected areas. Whether you're in the field or at your desk, SAPAA provides the tools you need to:

  • View all protected sites in one place
  • Track when sites were last inspected
  • Review inspection details and trends in naturalness scores
  • Monitor overall program health (Admins only)
  • Manage user accounts (Admins only)

docs.title: Who Should Use This Manual
docs.text:
This manual is written for:

  • Stewards: Field workers who visit and inspect protected areas
  • Coordinators: Program managers who oversee multiple sites
  • Administrators: System managers who handle user accounts and overall program health

You don't need to be technically experienced to use SAPAA. If you can browse websites and use a smartphone, you can use these applications.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
Mobile App:

  • Android 5.0 or iOS 10.0 or higher
  • At least 100MB of free storage space
  • Internet connection (for initial download and syncing)
  • GPS capability (for map features)

Web App:

  • Modern web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari)
  • Laptop or desktop computer recommended
  • Stable internet connection

docs.title: Creating Your Account
docs.text:
You can create your own SAPAA account or, in some deployments, have one created for you by an administrator.

Option 1: Self Sign-Up (Mobile or Web)

  1. Open the SAPAA mobile app or web app.
  2. On the login screen in the Web, under “Don’t have an account?”, click the “Create one” button. On the mobile App, click "Sign Up".
  3. Choose how you want to create your account:
  4. Continue with Google – sign up using your Google account.
  5. Continue with Microsoft – sign up using your Microsoft / organization account.
  6. Email and Password – enter your email address and choose a secure password.
  7. Follow the on-screen prompts to complete the sign-up process.
  8. After successful sign-up, you will be logged in or redirected to the login screen.

Option 2: Account Created by an Administrator

  1. Contact your SAPAA administrator or coordinator.
  2. They may create an account for you and provide:
    • Your login email address
    • A temporary password (if not using Google/Microsoft sign-in)
  3. You’ll receive login details via email or direct communication.

docs.title: Logging In
docs.text:
You can log in using Google, Microsoft, or email and password.

Mobile App:

  1. Open the SAPAA app on your device.
  2. On the login screen, choose one of the following:
    • Continue with Google – tap and select your Google account.
    • Continue with Microsoft – tap and sign in with your Microsoft / organization account.
    • Email and Password – enter your email address and password, then tap “Login”.
  3. If your credentials are valid, you’ll see the Analytics screen.

Web App:

  1. Open your web browser.
  2. Navigate to the SAPAA web app URL (provided by your coordinator).
  3. On the login page, choose one of the following:
    • Continue with Google – sign in using your Google account.
    • Continue with Microsoft – sign in using your Microsoft / organization account.
    • Email and Password – enter your email address and password, then click “Login”.
  4. If login is successful, you’ll be directed to the Protected Areas page.

First-Time Login Tip: If you received a temporary password, consider changing it to something memorable (contact your administrator for assistance).

docs.title: Understanding User Roles
docs.text:
There are two types of users in SAPAA:

Stewards (General Users):

  • View all protected sites
  • Read inspection details and history
  • Search and sort site information
  • Download sites for offline use (mobile app)
  • Generate site reports (mobile app)

Administrators:

  • All steward features, plus:
  • Access admin dashboard with program-wide statistics
  • View data visualizations and heatmaps
  • Create, edit, and delete user accounts
  • Manage user roles and permissions

How to Tell Your Role:

  • Mobile: Look for the "Admin Dashboard" option in the menu
  • Web: Look for the "Admin" button on the Protected Areas page

If you don't see admin features, you have a steward account.

docs.title: Installing the App
docs.text:
1. Open the Google Play Store (Android) or App Store (iOS)
  2. Search for "SAPAA" or the full name provided by your coordinator
  3. Tap "Install" or "Get"
  4. Wait for the download and installation to complete
  5. Tap "Open" to launch the app

docs.title: Navigating the Mobile App
docs.text:
The app has three main sections, accessible via tabs at the bottom of the screen:

  1. Analytics Tab (📊): View statistics, charts, and search for sites
  2. Sites Tab (📋): Browse, search, and manage the list of protected areas
  3. Map Tab (🗺️): View sites on an interactive map

Menu Options (Top Right, Three Dots):

  • Settings
  • Admin Dashboard (admins only)
  • Logout

docs.title: Viewing Site Analytics
docs.text:
Purpose: Get a quick overview of program status and site health.

Steps:

  1. Tap the Analytics tab at the bottom of the screen
  2. Review the dashboard sections:

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):

  • Total Sites Inspected: Number of sites with at least one inspection
  • Last Inspection: Date of the most recent inspection across all sites

Naturalness Score Distribution Chart:

  • A pie chart showing how sites are rated:
    • Excellent (green)
    • Good (light green)
    • Fair (yellow)
    • Poor (red)
    • Cannot Answer (gray)
  • Tap any slice to see the count for that category

Site Locator (Heatmap):

  1. Type a site name or keyword in the search bar
  2. Tap "Search"
  3. Sites matching your search appear on the map
  4. Toggle "Show Markers" to switch between heatmap view and individual pins

Refreshing Data:

  • Tap "Sync Now" to manually update all data from the server

docs.title: Browsing and Searching Sites
docs.text:
Purpose: Find and view protected areas you're interested in.

Steps:

  1. Tap the Sites tab at the bottom

Browsing the List:

  • Scroll through the list to see all available sites
  • Each site card shows:
  • Site name (bold)
  • Location/county
  • Last inspection date (or "No inspection date")
  • Status badge (color-coded):
    • 🟢 Recently Visited
    • 🟡 Visited This Year
    • 🟠 Visited Recently
    • 🔴 Needs Review

Searching for a Site:

  1. Tap the search bar at the top
  2. Type part of the site name or location
  3. The list updates automatically to show matching sites
  4. Clear the search to see all sites again

Sorting the List:

  1. Tap the "Sort" button
  2. Choose how you want to organize sites:
    • Name (A-Z): Alphabetical order
    • Name (Z-A): Reverse alphabetical
    • Newest First: Most recently inspected sites first
    • Oldest First: Least recently inspected sites first
  3. The list updates immediately

Understanding Online/Offline Status:

  • Look for the badge at the top of the Sites tab:
    • 🟢 Online: Connected to the internet, showing all sites
    • 🔴 Offline: No internet, showing only downloaded sites

docs.title: Working Offline
docs.text:
Purpose: Access site information when you're in remote areas without internet.

Downloading Sites for Offline Use:

  1. While online, go to the Sites tab
  2. Tap the checkbox (☐) next to each site you want to download
    • You can select multiple sites
  3. Tap the "Download" button (appears when sites are selected)
  4. Wait for the download to complete
  5. A checkmark (✓) appears next to downloaded sites

Using the App Offline:

  • When you lose internet connection, the app automatically switches to offline mode
  • Only downloaded sites will appear in your list
  • You can still:
    • View site details
    • Read inspection reports
    • Generate PDF reports

Managing Storage Space:

  1. Go to the Sites tab while offline
  2. Select sites you no longer need offline
  3. Tap the "Delete" button
  4. Confirm deletion
  5. This frees up space on your device

Tip: Download sites before heading to the field. Common strategies:

  • Download all sites in your assigned region
  • Download sites you plan to visit this month
  • Download frequently referenced sites

docs.title: Viewing Site Details (Mobile)
docs.text:
Purpose: Get complete information about a specific protected area.

Steps:

  1. From the Sites tab, tap on any site card
  2. The Site Detail screen opens

Overview Section:

  • Site Name (large heading)
  • Type: E.g., Provincial Park, Natural Area
  • Region: Geographic area
  • Area: Size in hectares
  • Recreational Activities: Available activities at the site

Naturalness Score:

  • Large number showing the average score (e.g., 3.4)
  • Color-coded slider:
    • Red (1.0): Poor condition
    • Yellow (2.0): Fair condition
    • Light Green (3.0): Good condition
    • Dark Green (4.0): Excellent condition
  • An indicator shows where this site's score falls on the scale

Viewing Inspection Reports:

There are two ways to view inspection data:

Option 1: By Date

  1. Tap "By Date" at the top of the reports section
  2. You'll see a list of all inspections, newest first
  3. Each inspection shows:
    • Inspection date
    • Naturalness score for that visit
  4. Tap any inspection to expand it and see:
    • Steward name
    • Naturalness details (comments)
    • Individual observations (Q1, Q2, Q3, etc.)

Option 2: By Question

  1. Tap "By Question"
  2. You'll see a list of all inspection questions (Q1, Q2, Q3, etc.)
  3. Each question shows:
    • Question ID and text
    • Number of responses across inspections
  4. Tap any question to see how answers have changed over time
  5. Responses are shown from newest to oldest

When to Use Each View:

  • Use "By Date" when you want to read a complete inspection report
  • Use "By Question" when you want to track changes to a specific aspect over time (e.g., "How has trail condition changed?")

Navigating Back:

  • Tap the back arrow (←) at the top left to return to the Sites list

docs.title: Using the Map
docs.text:
Purpose: See where protected areas are located geographically.

Steps:

  1. Tap the Map tab at the bottom
  2. The map loads showing all sites as pins
  3. Zoom in/out using pinch gestures
  4. Tap any pin to see the site name
  5. Tap "Open in Google Maps" to:
    • Launch the Google Maps app
    • Get navigation directions to the site

Requirements:

  • Internet connection (to load map tiles)
  • GPS enabled (for showing your current location)

Tip: Use this before a field visit to plan your route.

docs.title: Generating Reports
docs.text:
Purpose: Create a PDF summary of a site's status and inspection history.

Steps:

  1. Open any site's detail page
  2. Scroll to the bottom
  3. Tap the "Generate Report" button
  4. Wait a few seconds while the PDF is created
  5. The report opens in your device's PDF viewer
  6. From there, you can:
    • View the report on screen
    • Share it via email or messaging
    • Save it to your device

What's Included in the Report:

  • Site overview (name, type, region, area)
  • Naturalness score
  • List of all inspections with dates and scores
  • Detailed observations from recent inspections

Tip: Generate reports before going offline to have them available in the field.

docs.title: Managing Your Profile (Mobile)
docs.text:
Accessing Settings:

  1. Tap the three-dot menu (⋮) in the top right corner
  2. Select "Settings"

Logging Out:

  1. From the menu, tap "Logout"
  2. Confirm you want to sign out
  3. You'll return to the login screen

Admin Dashboard (Admins Only):

  1. From the menu, tap "Admin Dashboard"
  2. This opens admin-specific features (see Web Application Guide for details)

docs.title: Accessing the Web App
docs.text:
Steps:

  1. Open a modern web browser on your computer
    • Recommended: Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari
  2. Navigate to your SAPAA web app URL
    • This is provided by your coordinator (e.g., https://sapaa.yourorg.ca)
    • Bookmark this page for easy access
  3. If you don't have the URL or an account, contact your administrator

After Login:

  • You'll land on the Protected Areas page
  • This is your home screen

docs.title: Viewing Protected Areas
docs.text:
Purpose: See all sites at a glance and quickly identify which need attention.

Understanding the Header:

At the top of the Protected Areas page, you'll see:

  • Title: "Protected Areas" with the SAPAA logo
  • Tagline: "Monitor and track site inspections across Alberta"
  • Admin Button (top right, admins only): Access admin features

Quick Statistics Cards:

Four cards show program-wide stats:

  1. Total Sites: Total number of registered sites
  2. Total Inspections: Number of sites with at least one inspection
  3. Active This Year: Sites inspected in the past year
  4. Needs Attention: Sites not inspected for 2+ years

These give you an instant snapshot of program health.

Searching for a Site:

  1. Click in the search box below the statistics
  2. Type any part of a site name or county name
  3. The site list updates automatically
  4. A line below shows "X sites found"
  5. Clear the search box to see all sites again

Sorting Sites:

  1. Click the "Sort" button
  2. Select an option:
    • Name (A–Z): Alphabetical by site name
    • Name (Z–A): Reverse alphabetical
    • Most Recent: Sites with newest inspections first
    • Oldest First: Sites with oldest inspections first
  3. The list updates immediately

Reading Site Cards:

Each site appears as a card showing:

  • Site Name (bold)
  • County (with location icon 📍)
  • Inspection Date: Friendly format (e.g., "Apr 12, 2024") or "No inspection date"
  • Status Badge (color-coded):
    • 🟢 Recently Visited
    • 🟡 Visited This Year
    • 🟠 Visited Recently
    • 🔴 Needs Review
  • Last Visit Age: E.g., "10d ago", "3mo ago", "2yr ago"

Opening a Site:

  • Click anywhere on a site card
  • You'll navigate to the Site Detail page

docs.title: Exploring Site Details (Web)
docs.text:
Purpose: Get in-depth information about a single site and its inspection history.

Accessing Site Details:

  1. From the Protected Areas page, click on any site card
  2. The URL changes to /detail/[site-name]

Header Section:

  • Back to Sites button (←): Return to the main list
  • Site Name (large, bold)
  • County (with map pin 📍)
  • Last Inspection: Date displayed if available
  • Last Visit Badge: Time since last visit (e.g., "3mo ago")

Site Statistics Cards:

Three cards summarize the site:

  1. Total Reports: Number of inspection records
  2. Avg. Score: Average naturalness score (e.g., 3.2) or "N/A"
  3. Condition: Text label based on score:
    • Excellent
    • Good
    • Fair
    • Poor

Naturalness Score Gradient:

If scores are available, you'll see:

  • Large numeric value (e.g., 3.4)
  • Color gradient bar from red → yellow → green
  • Small indicator showing where this site's average falls
  • Scale labels:
    • 1.0 Poor
    • 2.0 Fair
    • 3.0 Good
    • 4.0 Excellent

Interpretation:

  • Toward red: Site is degraded or disturbed
  • Toward green: Site is in good/excellent natural condition

docs.title: Comparing Inspection Data
docs.text:
Purpose: Analyze inspection history in two different ways.

Switching Views:

Below the Naturalness Score section, you'll see two buttons:

  • View by Date
  • Compare by Question

Click either to switch between viewing modes.

View by Date:

When to Use: Read complete inspection reports one at a time.

What You'll See:

  • Heading: "Inspection Reports (X)" where X is the total count
  • Each inspection is a collapsible card showing:
    • Inspection date
    • Naturalness score for that visit (or "N/A")

Steps:

  1. Click "View by Date"
  2. Click on any inspection card to expand it
  3. You'll see:
    • Steward Name: Who conducted the inspection
    • Naturalness Details: Free-text comments about site condition
    • Observations: Individual question responses (Q1, Q2, Q3, etc.)
  4. Each observation shows:
    • Question ID
    • Answer text from that inspection

Tip: Use this to read through what a steward observed on a specific date.

Compare by Question:

When to Use: Track how a specific aspect has changed over time.

What You'll See:

  • Heading: "Question Comparison (X questions)"
  • Each question is a card showing:
    • Question ID (e.g., Q3) in a badge
    • Question text/label
    • Number of responses (e.g., "3 responses across inspections")

Steps:

  1. Click "Compare by Question"
  2. Click on any question card to expand it
  3. You'll see all responses to that question, sorted newest to oldest
  4. Each response shows:
    • Inspection date
    • Answer text

Example Use Case:

  • Question: "Trail condition"
  • You can see: "Good" (2024), "Fair" (2023), "Good" (2022)
  • This shows the trail has been mostly well-maintained

Tip: Use this to identify trends or recurring issues at a site.

docs.title: Using the Admin Dashboard
docs.text:
Purpose: Monitor program-wide statistics and locate sites geographically.

Who Can Access: Administrators only

Steps to Access:

  1. From the Protected Areas page, click the "Admin" button (top right)
  2. You'll navigate to /admin/dashboard

Dashboard Overview:

The dashboard has four main sections:

1. Header & Summary Stats:

Two cards at the top show:

  • Total Records: Total inspection records in the database
  • Last Record: Date of the most recent inspection

2. Naturalness Distribution (Pie Chart):

  • Shows how all inspections are distributed across naturalness categories
  • Each slice represents:
    • Excellent
    • Good
    • Fair
    • Poor
    • Cannot Answer
  • Slice colors match the condition (green for good, red for poor, etc.)

Use This To:

  • See overall site health across the program
  • Identify if most sites are in good condition or need attention

3. Top 5 Sites (Pie Chart):

  • Shows the five sites with the most inspection records
  • Each slice shows:
    • Site name
    • Total inspection count

Use This To:

  • Identify heavily monitored sites
  • See which sites get the most attention from stewards

4. Site Heatmap (Keyword Search + Map):

Purpose: Locate sites geographically based on search terms.

Steps:

  1. Type a keyword in the search box (e.g., "river", "park", part of a site name)
  2. Click "Search"
  3. The system:
    • Searches for matching sites in the database
    • Counts inspection records for each site
    • Uses geocoding (OpenCage API) to find coordinates
    • Plots sites on the map as a heatmap or markers

What You'll See:

  • If sites are found: Map with colored points or heatmap
  • Below the search bar: "Found X locations for 'keyword'"
  • If no matches: "No sites found"
  • If geocoding fails: Message about locations not being available

Tips:

  • Use short keywords (e.g., "lake" instead of "lakeside recreation area")
  • Try different variations if results are unexpected
  • Use this to identify geographic clusters of certain site types

docs.title: Managing User Accounts
docs.text:
Purpose: Add, edit, and remove user accounts.

Who Can Access: Administrators only

Steps to Access:

  1. Navigate to /admin/account-management
    • Or click the link from the admin menu if available

Header & Summary:

At the top, you'll see:

  • Title: "Account Management"
  • Subtitle: "Manage user accounts and permissions"
  • Add User button (top right)

Three summary cards show:

  1. Total Users: Number of accounts
  2. Admins: Count of administrator accounts
  3. Stewards: Count of steward accounts

Searching for a User:

  1. Click in the search bar labeled "Search by email..."
  2. Type any part of a user's email address
  3. The user list filters automatically
  4. Clear the search to see all users

Filtering and Sorting Users:

  1. Click the "Filter & Sort" button
  2. Choose an option:
    • All Users
    • Email (A–Z)
    • Email (Z–A)
    • Stewards Only
    • Admins Only
  3. The user list updates immediately

Understanding User Cards:

Each user appears as a card showing:

  • Avatar: Circle with first letter of email
  • Email Address
  • Role Badge:
    • Admin
    • Steward

Editing an Existing User:

  1. Click on any user card
  2. An "Account Details" modal opens
  3. You can update:
    • Email address
    • Role (admin or steward)
    • Password (if supported)
  4. Click "Save" to confirm changes
  5. The user list updates

Adding a New User:

  1. Click the "Add User" button (top right)
  2. Fill in the required fields:
    • Email: User's email address
    • Role: Select "Admin" or "Steward"
    • Password: Set an initial password (if required)
  3. Click "Save"
  4. The new user appears in the list
  5. Important: Share login credentials with the new user securely
    • Encourage them to change their password if possible

Deleting a User:

  1. Click on a user card to open the edit modal
  2. Click the "Delete" option (if visible)
  3. Confirm you want to delete the user
  4. The user is removed from the list

Warning: Deleted users can no longer log in. Use this carefully.

Best Practices:

  • Regularly review user accounts and remove inactive users
  • Assign admin role only to trusted personnel
  • Use secure, unique passwords for each account
  • Document who has admin access for accountability

docs.title: Common Tasks
docs.text: This section walks through common tasks you'll perform, regardless of which application you're using.

docs.title: Finding a Specific Site
docs.text:
Scenario: You need to look up "Elk Island Provincial Park" to check its status.

Mobile App:

  1. Open the app and go to the Sites tab
  2. Tap the search bar at the top
  3. Type "Elk Island"
  4. The site appears in the filtered list
  5. Tap the site card to view details

Web App:

  1. Log in and go to the Protected Areas page
  2. Click in the search box
  3. Type "Elk Island"
  4. The site appears in the filtered list
  5. Click the site card to view details

Tip: You can search by partial name or by county/location.

docs.title: Checking When a Site Was Last Inspected
docs.text:
Scenario: Your supervisor asks when "Writing-on-Stone" was last visited.

Mobile App:

  1. Search for "Writing-on-Stone" in the Sites tab
  2. Look at the site card:
    • Inspection date is shown (e.g., "Sep 15, 2024")
    • Or "No inspection date" if never inspected
    • Last visit age (e.g., "2mo ago")

Web App:

  1. Search for "Writing-on-Stone" on the Protected Areas page
  2. Look at the site card:
    • Inspection date is shown
    • Or "No inspection date"
    • Last visit age (e.g., "2mo ago")

Alternative: Open the site detail page for more information.

docs.title: Reviewing Inspection History
docs.text:
Scenario: You want to see all past inspections for a site to identify trends.

Mobile App:

  1. Open the site's detail page
  2. Scroll to the Inspection Reports section
  3. Choose your view:
    • By Date: See each inspection in chronological order
    • By Question: See how specific questions were answered over time
  4. Tap any inspection or question to expand and read details

Web App:

  1. Open the site's detail page
  2. Scroll to the inspection section
  3. Toggle between:
    • View by Date: Read complete reports
    • Compare by Question: Track changes to specific aspects
  4. Click to expand and review details

Tip: Use "By Question" to spot trends like increasing trail erosion or improving naturalness.

docs.title: Identifying Sites That Need Attention
docs.text:
Scenario: You're planning field visits and want to prioritize sites that haven't been inspected recently.

Mobile App:

  1. Go to the Sites tab
  2. Tap "Sort"
  3. Select "Oldest First"
  4. Sites that haven't been inspected (or inspected longest ago) appear at the top
  5. Look for sites with:
    • 🔴 "Needs Review" badge
    • "2yr ago" or longer in the last visit age

Web App:

  1. Go to the Protected Areas page
  2. Look at the "Needs Attention" stat card
    • Shows count of sites not inspected for 2+ years
  3. Click "Sort" → "Oldest First"
  4. Sites needing attention appear at the top
  5. Look for sites with:
    • 🔴 "Needs Review" badge
    • Old last visit dates

Tip: Combine with search to find sites in your assigned region that need visits.

docs.title: Understanding Naturalness Scores
docs.text:
What is a Naturalness Score?

The Naturalness Score is an average value (typically 1.0 to 4.0) that summarizes how natural or disturbed a site is based on inspection reports.

Score Ranges:

  • 1.0 – Poor: Highly disturbed, significant human impact
  • 2.0 – Fair: Some disturbance, but still functional
  • 3.0 – Good: Mostly natural with minor disturbances
  • 4.0 – Excellent: Pristine, natural condition

How It's Calculated:

  • Each inspection includes a naturalness rating
  • The score shown is the average of all inspections
  • "N/A" means no scores have been recorded

Why It Matters:

  • Helps identify sites that may need restoration or management
  • Tracks whether site condition is improving or declining over time
  • Guides resource allocation for conservation efforts

Where to Find It:

Mobile App:

  • On any site detail page, under the overview section
  • Color-coded slider shows where the score falls

Web App:

  • On any site detail page, in the statistics cards
  • Gradient bar visualizes the score from red (poor) to green (excellent)

Interpreting Trends:

  • If scores are declining: Site may be experiencing increased impact
  • If scores are improving: Management actions may be working
  • Use "Compare by Question" to see what specifically is changing

docs.title: Mobile App Issues
docs.text:
Problem: App Won't Open or Crashes on Launch

Possible Causes:

  • Corrupted installation
  • Insufficient storage space
  • Outdated operating system

Solutions:

  1. Restart your device
  2. Check for app updates:
    • Open the App Store or Play Store
    • Search for SAPAA
    • Tap "Update" if available
  3. Check storage space:
    • Go to device Settings → Storage
    • Free up space if needed
  4. Reinstall the app:
    • Uninstall SAPAA
    • Restart your device
    • Reinstall from the store
    • Log in again

Problem: Cannot Log In

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect email or password
  • Account not created yet
  • Network connection issue

Solutions:

  1. Double-check your credentials:
    • Ensure Caps Lock is off
    • Verify email is spelled correctly
  2. Check your internet connection:
    • Try loading a website in your browser
    • Connect to Wi-Fi if available
  3. Contact your administrator:
    • They can verify your account exists
    • They can reset your password if needed

Problem: Sites Not Loading or Showing "No Sites Found"

Possible Causes:

  • No internet connection
  • No sites downloaded for offline use
  • Server issue

Solutions:

  1. Check online/offline status:
    • Look at the badge at the top of the Sites tab
    • If offline, only downloaded sites appear
  2. Try syncing:
    • Go to the Analytics tab
    • Tap "Sync Now"
    • Wait for data to refresh
  3. Download sites for offline use (see "Working Offline" section)
  4. If problem persists: Contact your administrator

Problem: Map Not Loading

Possible Causes:

  • No internet connection
  • GPS disabled
  • Map service issue

Solutions:

  1. Check internet connection: Map tiles require internet
  2. Enable GPS:
    • Go to device Settings → Location
    • Turn on location services
    • Allow SAPAA to access location
  3. Try again later: Map services occasionally have outages

Problem: Cannot Generate PDF Report

Possible Causes:

  • Insufficient device permissions
  • Low storage space
  • Missing data

Solutions:

  1. Check app permissions:
    • Go to device Settings → Apps → SAPAA
    • Ensure storage permissions are granted
  2. Free up storage space
  3. Ensure the site has inspection data: Reports require at least one inspection

docs.title: Web App Issues
docs.text:
Problem: "Unable to Load Sites" Error

Possible Causes:

  • Internet connection lost
  • Server maintenance
  • Database issue

Solutions:

  1. Check your internet connection:
    • Try loading another website
    • Refresh the page
  2. Click "Try Again" on the error screen
  3. Clear browser cache:
    • Chrome: Settings → Privacy → Clear browsing data
    • Select "Cached images and files"
    • Click "Clear data"
  4. If problem persists: Contact your administrator

Problem: "Unable to Load Site" on Detail Page

Possible Causes:

  • Site was deleted
  • Database error
  • Incorrect URL

Solutions:

  1. Click "Back to Sites"
  2. Search for the site again
  3. If it doesn't appear: The site may have been removed
  4. Contact your administrator if you believe the site should exist

Problem: Cannot Log In

Possible Causes:

  • Incorrect credentials
  • Browser cookies disabled
  • Account issue

Solutions:

  1. Verify email and password:
    • Ensure Caps Lock is off
    • Check for typos
  2. Enable cookies:
    • Browser Settings → Privacy
    • Allow cookies for the SAPAA site
  3. Try a different browser
  4. Contact your administrator for password reset

docs.title: Problem: Admin Dashboard Shows "No Data Available"
docs.text:
Possible Causes:

  • No inspection data in database
  • Database permissions issue
  • Chart rendering error

Solutions:

  • Refresh the page
  • Check other admin features:
  • If Account Management works, it's likely a data issue
  • Try the heatmap search:
  • Search for a known site
  • If it works, the database is accessible
  • Contact your administrator if charts remain empty

docs.title: Problem: Heatmap Returns "No Sites Found"
docs.text:
Possible Causes:

  • Search term doesn't match any sites
  • Geocoding service issue
  • Database query error

Solutions:

  • Try different keywords:
    • Use shorter terms (e.g., "lake" instead of "lakeside park")
    • Try partial names
  • Check spelling
  • Try a site you know exists
  • If the map appears but is empty: geocoding may have failed for those sites

docs.title: Problem: I Don't See the "Admin" Button
docs.text:
Cause: Your account has the steward role, not admin.

Solution:

  • Ask an existing admin to check your role
  • Go to Account Management (if you have another admin account)
  • Edit your user and change role to Admin

docs.title: Problem: Mobile App Shows Old Data
docs.text:
Cause: Data hasn't synced recently.

Solution:

  1. Go to the Analytics tab
  2. Tap "Sync Now"
  3. Wait for the sync to complete
  4. Check the "Last Inspection" date to confirm it updated

docs.title: Problem: Changes in Web App Don't Appear in Mobile App
docs.text:
Cause: Mobile app data is cached and needs to be refreshed.

Solution:

  1. Open the mobile app
  2. Go to Analytics → "Sync Now"
  3. This pulls the latest data from the server

docs.title: Problem: Offline Data is Outdated
docs.text:
Cause: Downloaded sites were cached before recent updates.

Solution:

  1. Connect to the internet
  2. Go to the Sites tab
  3. Select the outdated sites
  4. Delete them from offline storage
  5. Download them again (this gets the latest data)

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text:
Q1: What's the difference between a steward and an admin?

A:

  • Stewards can view all sites, read inspection details, search and sort data, and generate reports (mobile app).
  • Admins have all steward features plus access to the Admin Dashboard (statistics, heatmap) and Account Management (create/edit/delete users).

Q2: What does "Needs Attention" mean?

A: A site is flagged "Needs Attention" (or "Needs Review") when it hasn't been inspected for approximately 2 years or more. This signals to coordinators that the site may need a new visit.

Q3: Why do some sites say "No inspection date"?

A: This means no inspection date has been recorded in the database for that site. Possible reasons:

  • It's a newly added site
  • It's an older record without a date
  • Inspections were conducted but dates weren't entered

Q4: What is the Naturalness Score?

A: The Naturalness Score is an average value (1.0 to 4.0) summarizing how natural or disturbed a site is based on inspection reports:

  • 4.0 = Excellent: Pristine, natural condition
  • 3.0 = Good: Mostly natural, minor disturbances
  • 2.0 = Fair: Some disturbance
  • 1.0 = Poor: Highly disturbed

Higher values indicate better natural conditions.

Q5: Can I change my own password?

A: This depends on your deployment configuration. If you don't see a "Change Password" or "Reset Password" option in the app or web interface, contact your SAPAA administrator for assistance.

Q6: How do I download sites for offline use?

A: (Mobile app only)

  1. Go to the Sites tab while online
  2. Tap the checkbox next to sites you want to download
  3. Tap "Download"
  4. Sites are now available offline

Q7: Why doesn't a site show up on the heatmap?

A: Possible reasons:

  • The site name didn't match your search keyword
  • The site couldn't be geocoded (no coordinates found)
  • Temporary issue with the geocoding service

Try a different keyword or contact an admin.

Q8: How often should I sync the mobile app?

A: It depends on your workflow:

  • Daily field use: Sync daily or before heading out
  • Occasional use: Sync weekly or before important meetings
  • After major updates: Sync immediately if you know new data was added

Q9: Can I use the mobile app without internet?

A: Yes, but with limitations:

  • You must download sites while online first
  • Only downloaded sites are available offline
  • You can view details, read inspections, and generate reports
  • You cannot sync new data or access the map

Q10: Who do I contact if I have a problem?

A: Contact your SAPAA program coordinator or system administrator using the contact information provided by your organization.

Q11: How do I know which version of the app I'm using?

A: (Mobile app)

  • Go to device Settings → Apps → SAPAA
  • The version number is listed there

Q12: Can I access SAPAA from a tablet?

A:

  • Mobile app: Yes, if you have an Android or iOS tablet
  • Web app: Yes, any device with a modern web browser can access it

Q13: What happens if I lose internet connection while using the web app?

A: You'll see error messages when trying to load data. Reconnect to continue. The mobile app is better suited for offline field work.

Q14: How do I report a bug or suggest a feature?

A: Contact your SAPAA coordinator with details about:

  • What you were trying to do
  • What happened instead
  • Which device/browser you were using

docs.title: Contact Information
docs.text:
For help with accounts, data issues, or technical problems:

Contact SAPAA at https://sapaastewards.com/contact-us/.

For Urgent Field Support:

  • Save offline sites before heading to remote areas
  • Contact your coordinator before departures if you anticipate issues

Feedback and Suggestions:

  • We welcome your input to improve SAPAA
  • Share your ideas with your coordinator

Document Version: 1.0 Last Updated: November 2025 Prepared for: Stewards of Alberta's Protected Areas Association

Thank you for using SAPAA to protect Alberta's natural areas.

docs.title: API Documentation From Winter 2026
docs.text:
Interactive API documentation is available through Swagger.

You can view and test all API endpoints here:

Swagger API Documentation

Swagger (Local): http://localhost:3000/api-docs

You can explore the API, view request/response formats, and test endpoints directly from the browser.

docs.title: For a detailed explanation of the API endpoints see:
docs.text: Media Storage Architecture

docs.title: API Documentation From Fall 2025
docs.text: The web application exposes the following API endpoints:

docs.title: 1. Geocoding API
docs.text:
Endpoint: GET /api/geocode

Description: Geocodes a location name to coordinates using OpenCage API.

Query Parameters: - q (required): Location name to geocode

Example Request:

GET /api/geocode?q=Elk Island Provincial Park

Example Response:

{
  "latitude": 53.5731,
  "longitude": -112.8583
}

Error Response:

{
  "error": "Missing q param"
}

Status: 400

Rate Limits: - Limited by OpenCage API quota (2,500 requests/day on free tier)

docs.title: 2. Heatmap API
docs.text:
Endpoint: GET /api/heatmap

Description: Searches for sites matching a keyword and returns counts for heatmap visualization.

Query Parameters: - keyword (optional): Search term to match against site names

Example Request:

GET /api/heatmap?keyword=park

Example Response:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "namesite": "Elk Island Provincial Park",
      "count": 15
    },
    {
      "namesite": "Writing-on-Stone Provincial Park",
      "count": 12
    }
  ]
}

Empty Response:

{
  "data": [],
  "message": "No sites found"
}

Error Response:

{
  "error": "Database query failed"
}

Status: 400 or 500

Authentication: - Requires authenticated user session (handled by middleware)

docs.title: 3. Authentication Endpoints
docs.text:
These endpoints are handled by Next.js and Supabase:

  • POST /login - User login
  • POST /signup - User registration
  • GET /auth/callback - OAuth callback handler
  • GET /auth/confirm - Email confirmation handler

Note: These are internal API routes. Use the frontend login/signup pages instead of calling these directly.

docs.title: Media Storage Architecture
docs.text: This document describes how images are uploaded, stored, and retrieved in the SAPAA SIR system. The goal is to help future development teams understand how media files flow through the system and how AWS S3 is used.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Images uploaded through the SAPAA application are stored in AWS S3, while their metadata is stored in the Supabase PostgreSQL database.

The system uses presigned URLs so that users upload files directly to S3 instead of routing large files through the application server.

This approach improves:

  • performance
  • scalability
  • security

docs.title: Step 1 – User selects an image
docs.text:
A user uploads an image through:

  • through a Site Inspection Report (SIR) or
  • through the Standalone Image Upload feature (to be implemented in Sprint-4)

The user may enter metadata such as:

  • caption
  • description
  • identifier (Sprint-4)
  • photographer (Sprint-4)

docs.title: Step 2 – Client requests a presigned upload URL
docs.text:
The frontend sends a request to:

POST /api/s3/presign

The request contains:

  • filename
  • contentType
  • fileSize
  • siteId
  • responseId
  • questionId

The API validates:

  • user authentication
  • file size
  • allowed file types
  • required metadata

docs.title: S3 Storage Structure
docs.text:
Images are stored in the S3 bucket: sapaa-inspection-images

Objects are stored using the following structure: inspections/{siteId}/{responseId}/{questionId}/{uuid}.jpg

Example: inspections/207/3235/27/a1b2c3d4.jpg

docs.title: Why a UUID is added
docs.text:
Even if two images have identical metadata, the UUID prevents file collisions.

This ensures files are never overwritten.

docs.title: Database Metadata Storage
docs.text:
Image metadata is stored in the table: W26_attachments

Important fields include:

Field Description id Attachment ID site_id Associated site response_id Associated SIR response question_id Question the image belongs to filename SAPAA formatted filename storage_key S3 object key caption Image caption description Optional description file_size_bytes File size content_type MIME type

docs.title: Gallery API Endpoints
docs.text:
This section documents the API endpoints used to retrieve uploaded images and their metadata.

Images are stored in AWS S3 while metadata is stored in the Supabase PostgreSQL database. These endpoints generate temporary signed URLs so that images can be securely accessed by authenticated users.

docs.title: API Access Control
docs.text: Some API endpoints in the SAPAA system are restricted based on user roles. In particular, the gallery administration endpoint is only accessible to users with the admin role.

docs.title: GET /api/gallery
docs.text:
This endpoint returns all uploaded images across all sites along with their associated metadata.

Because this endpoint exposes all media files and metadata in the system, it is protected with a server-side authorization check.

docs.title: Access Level
docs.text: Only users whose role is set to admin in their authentication metadata are allowed to access this endpoint.

docs.title: Authorization Logic
docs.text:
When a request is received, the server:

  1. Retrieves the currently authenticated user using Supabase authentication.
  2. Reads the user's role from their authentication metadata.
  3. Verifies that the role is admin.
  4. Returns a 403 Forbidden response if the user is not an administrator.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
Returns a JSON object containing a list of images and their metadata.

Example response:

{
  "items": [
    {
      "id": 14,
      "response_id": 3235,
      "question_id": 27,
      "caption": "Cracked Tree",
      "filename": "ClydeFen-2025-01-23-BobSuruncle-ATVTrack.jpg",
      "content_type": "image/jpeg",
      "file_size_bytes": 2433304,
      "description": "Large crack running up the trunk of a tree.",
      "storage_key": "inspections/207/3226/27/4c88c01f-8afb-4085-9140-c3bc41e3e00d.jpg",
      "site_id": 207,
      "site_name": "Riverlot 56 (NA)",
      "imageUrl": "https://sapaa-inspection-images.s3.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com/..."
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: GET /api/sites/{siteId}/gallery
docs.text:
Returns images associated with a specific site or inspection response.

This endpoint allows authenticated users to retrieve media files linked to a site inspection or site record.

docs.title: Access Level
docs.text:
Authenticated users only.

Unlike /api/gallery, which is restricted to administrators, this endpoint allows normal users to view images associated with sites they have access to.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
Returns a JSON object containing a list of images and their metadata.

Example response:

{
  "items": [
    {
      "id": 14,
      "caption": "Cracked Tree",
      "filename": "ClydeFen-2025-01-23-BobSuruncle-ATVTrack.jpg",
      "file_size_bytes": 2433304,
      "description": "Large crack running up the trunk of a tree.",
      "site_name": "Riverlot 56 (NA)",
      "imageUrl": "https://sapaa-inspection-images.s3.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com/..."
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: GET /api/site-images
docs.text:
Returns image attachments associated with a site or inspection response.

Images are stored in AWS S3 while metadata is stored in the Supabase database. This endpoint generates temporary signed URLs that allow images to be securely viewed.

docs.title: Access Level
docs.text:
Authenticated users only.

The server verifies that the requester is logged in before returning any image metadata.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text:
Parameter Type Required Description siteid integer No Filters images belonging to a specific site responseid integer No Filters images belonging to a specific inspection response

At least one parameter must be provided.

docs.title: Example Requests
docs.text:
Retrieve images for a site: /api/site-images?siteid=207

Retrieve images for an inspection response: /api/site-images?responseid=3235

Retrieve images filtered by both: /api/site-images?siteid=207&responseid=3235

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
Returns a JSON object containing an array of images.

Example:

{
  "items": [
    {
      "id": 14,
      "response_id": 3235,
      "question_id": 27,
      "filename": "ClydeFen-2025-01-23-BobSuruncle-ATVTrack.jpg",
      "storage_key": "inspections/207/3235/27/ClydeFen-2025-01-23-BobSuruncle-ATVTrack-a1b2c3d4.jpg",
      "content_type": "image/jpeg",
      "file_size_bytes": 2433304,
      "caption": "Cracked Tree",
      "description": "Large crack running up the trunk of a tree.",
      "site_id": 207,
      "imageUrl": "https://sapaa-inspection-images.s3.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com/..."
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Security Model
docs.text: The system protects images using several mechanisms:

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Only authenticated users can:

  • upload images
  • request presigned URLs

docs.title: Private S3 bucket
docs.text:
The S3 bucket is not publicly accessible.

Images are accessed through temporary signed URLs. This endpoint generates temporary signed URLs for each image using AWS S3. These URLs expire after 1 hour, preventing public access to stored images.

docs.title: Signed URLs
docs.text:
Two types of signed URLs are used:

Type Purpose Upload URL Allows client to upload file to S3 Download URL Allows temporary viewing of image

Signed URLs expire after a short time.

docs.title: Why Direct S3 Uploads Are Used
docs.text: Uploading files directly to S3 provides several benefits:

docs.title: Performance
docs.text: Large files do not pass through the application server.

docs.title: Scalability
docs.text: The backend does not become a bottleneck when many users upload images.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: The server validates requests before issuing upload permissions.

docs.title: Admin Gallery
docs.text:
Admins can view all uploaded images through the gallery interface.

Features include:

  • viewing images
  • viewing metadata
  • viewing storage path
  • inspecting captions and descriptions

Images are displayed using signed S3 URLs generated by the server.

docs.title: Related API Endpoints
docs.text: Endpoint Purpose /api/s3/presign Generate S3 upload URL /api/gallery Retrieve all images /api/sites/site-images Retrieve images for an inspection response /api/sites/{siteId}/gallery Retrieve images for a specific site

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
The SAPAA SIR system stores images in AWS S3 while maintaining metadata in Supabase.

Key features of the architecture include:

  • direct client uploads using presigned URLs
  • structured storage paths
  • standardized file naming
  • secure private storage
  • scalable image retrieval

This architecture allows SAPAA to efficiently store and organize large numbers of inspection images while maintaining strong security and performance

docs.title: Design System Reference for Development Teams
docs.text: Next.js · Tailwind CSS · Supabase · AWS

docs.title: 1.1 Purpose
docs.text:
This document defines the complete UI/UX design system for the SAPAA (Stewards of Alberta's Protected Areas Association) web application. It covers design principles, usability heuristics, accessibility considerations, and component-level implementation guidance for both the web (Next.js) and mobile (React Native) platforms.

Consistency is critical. Every page must follow the patterns described here so that users experience a coherent interface across all screens and workflows.

docs.title: 1.2 Technology Stack
docs.text: Area Technology Framework Next.js (App Router, TypeScript) Styling Tailwind CSS - utility-first, inline class names only Database Supabase (PostgreSQL) - tables prefixed W26_ Auth Supabase Auth with ProtectedRoute wrapper Drag and Drop @dnd-kit/core + @dnd-kit/sortable Maps Leaflet (SSR-guarded with mounted state) Charts Chart.js via react-chartjs-2 Icons lucide-react

docs.title: 1.3 Application Pages
docs.text: Route Component /sites HomeClient - public site listing /detail/[namesite] SiteDetailScreen - single site view /detail/[namesite]/new-report New inspection report form /detail/[namesite]/edit-report/[responseId] Edit existing report /login /signup Authentication pages /admin/dashboard Admin analytics and stats /admin/account-management User account administration /admin/sites Site management /admin/gallery Inspection photo gallery /admin/form-editor Form section and question editor

docs.title: 2.1 Visual Consistency
docs.text:
Maintain a consistent visual language to reduce cognitive load and build user confidence.

Unified navigation shell: - Mobile: All screens use the same green top app bar with title and back button. A persistent bottom tab bar provides access to Analytics, Sites, and SAPAA Map. - Web: Consistent header with SAPAA logo, gradient green background, and navigation elements. Admin pages use a hamburger menu for navigation.

Standardised colour palette: Primary green (#254431, #356B43) for headers and primary actions. White (#FFFFFF) for content cards. Light grey (#F7F2EA, #E4EBE4) for dividers and secondary surfaces. Red (#B91C1C) for destructive actions.

Typography hierarchy: Page titles at large and bold (24–32px). Section headers at medium and semi-bold (18–20px). Body text at regular weight (14–16px). Supporting text at smaller regular (12–14px).

docs.title: 2.2 Clear Visual Hierarchy
docs.text:
Guide attention using size, weight, colour, and spacing.

Card-based layout: Major information chunks are grouped into cards (e.g., Site Details, Naturalness Details, Analytics charts, Account tiles). Cards use consistent padding, rounded corners, and subtle shadows.

Strategic emphasis: Primary actions use filled green buttons with high visual weight (e.g., Preview PDF, Sync Now, Login). Secondary actions use outlined buttons or lower-contrast styling.

Consistent spacing: Padding inside cards (16–24px) and consistent vertical spacing between sections (16–32px) creates rhythm and improves readability.

docs.title: 2.3 Progressive Disclosure
docs.text:
Show only what users need at each step, and reveal more detail on demand.

Inspection Reports: Tabs for By Date and By Question instead of one long view. Expandable sections for detailed observations.

Site Details: High-level information at the top (name, location, key metrics). Detailed observations and naturalness details appear further down in separate cards.

Admin Features: Admin-specific features are not visible to regular users. Admin navigation is accessible via menu or dedicated button.

docs.title: 2.4 Action-Oriented Design
docs.text:
Make key tasks obvious and easy to complete.

Clear primary action per screen: Preview PDF on the report modal (mobile). Sync Now on Analytics (mobile). Search on the Sites/Protected Areas page. Add User on Account Management.

Button hierarchy: Primary uses solid green, often full width. Secondary uses outlined or low-emphasis styling. Destructive uses red with a clear label.

Immediate feedback: Counters and indicators update live as users interact. Loading states show progress indicators. Lists and cards visually respond to user interaction.

docs.title: 3.1 Visibility of System Status
docs.text:
The system always keeps users informed about what is going on.

Mobile: A field counter in the PDF modal updates live as users toggle checkboxes. The app bar title reflects the current screen. Bottom navigation highlights the active tab. An Online/Offline badge shows connection status.

Web: Loading spinners appear during data fetches. Page titles reflect the current location. Active navigation items are highlighted. Search results show a count of sites found.

Why this matters: Users can see that their actions are working and understand where they are in the app, reducing confusion and frustration.

docs.title: 3.2 Match Between System and the Real World
docs.text:
The application uses terminology that stewards already know: Naturalness Score, Recreational Activities, Observations. The SAPAA Map uses Google Maps (mobile) and Leaflet (web) with familiar map interactions. Inspection questions are labelled with codes (Q52, Q62, etc.) that match existing steward workflows.

Why this matters: Familiar language and visuals reduce training time and make the app feel like a natural extension of existing workflows.

docs.title: 3.3 User Control and Freedom
docs.text:
Provide clearly marked exits and ways to undo actions.

Mobile: A back arrow appears on every top bar. Modals can be dismissed with an X or the system back gesture. Users can toggle PDF fields freely, use Select All and Clear All, and preview a report before sharing it.

Web: Back buttons appear on all detail pages. Modal dialogs can be closed with the X button or by clicking outside. Cancel buttons appear on forms. Breadcrumb navigation is used where applicable.

Why this matters: Users feel safe exploring features because they know they can easily back out or adjust their choices.

docs.title: 3.4 Consistency and Standards
docs.text:
Follow platform conventions and maintain internal consistency.

Mobile: Consistent bottom navigation layout across all screens. Primary actions are always solid green; destructive actions are always red. List items follow the same pattern: icon left, label and detail text right.

Web: Consistent header design across all pages. Button styles are standardised (primary, secondary, destructive). Form inputs follow the same styling. Card components are reused throughout.

Why this matters: Once users learn basic patterns, they can apply them everywhere in the app.

docs.title: 3.5 Error Prevention
docs.text:
Design to prevent errors before they happen.

Mobile: The PDF flow separates Preview PDF and Share PDF so users do not accidentally share a report before checking it. Destructive actions such as Delete Account are clearly styled in red. Many inputs are constrained to checkboxes and predefined fields rather than free text for critical data.

Web: Form validation prevents invalid submissions. Confirmation dialogs guard destructive actions. Disabled states prevent invalid interactions. Clear error messages guide users to a resolution.

Why this matters: Preventing mistakes saves time and protects data integrity.

docs.title: 3.6 Recognition Rather Than Recall
docs.text:
All fields are clearly labelled: Region, Area (HA/AC), Naturalness Details, and so on. Bottom tabs (mobile) are always visible with both icons and text labels. PDF field selection mirrors the Site Details layout and naming. Status badges show inspection recency with colour coding so users do not need to calculate dates themselves.

Why this matters: Users do not have to remember information across screens; they can recognise it instead.

docs.title: 3.7 Flexibility and Efficiency of Use
docs.text:
Provide accelerators for expert users while keeping the interface simple for novices.

Bulk actions: Select All / Clear All for PDF fields (mobile). Sync Now for on-demand data refresh (mobile). Bulk selection for offline downloads (mobile).

Multiple access paths: Inspection reports can be viewed By Date (chronological workflow) or By Question (comparison/analysis workflow).

Smart defaults: PDF generation starts with all fields selected, so users typically only need to deselect a few. Search is always available. Sort options are remembered.

Why this matters: New users can follow straightforward flows, while experienced users can speed up their work with bulk actions and shortcuts.

docs.title: 3.8 Aesthetic and Minimalist Design
docs.text:
Interfaces should not contain irrelevant or rarely needed information.

Each screen is focused on one main task: view analytics, inspect a site, manage accounts, or generate a PDF. A limited colour palette (green, white, grey, and red for warnings) keeps the interface clean. Cards group only related information with enough white space for breathing room. Icons are used only where they add meaning, not for decoration.

Why this matters: A clean interface makes it easier to focus on what matters and reduces cognitive overload.

docs.title: 3.9 Help Users Recognise, Diagnose, and Recover from Errors
docs.text:
Error messages should be expressed in plain language, indicate the problem, and suggest a solution.

Mobile: Error messages appear as snackbars or toasts with clear explanations. Network errors show retry options. Validation errors appear inline with form fields.

Web: Error messages are displayed in red with clear visibility. Form validation shows specific field errors. Network errors provide retry buttons. 404 pages guide users back to main content.

Why this matters: Users can quickly understand what went wrong and how to fix it rather than feeling frustrated.

docs.title: 3.10 Help and Documentation
docs.text:
Help should be easy to find, focused on the user's task, and list concrete steps.

A user manual is available as a separate document. In-app tooltips and hints are provided where appropriate. Clear labels and placeholders guide users through forms. Status messages explain what actions do (e.g., "Syncing data..."). Page titles and descriptions provide contextual orientation throughout the web application.

Why this matters: While the interface should be self-explanatory, having documentation available helps users learn advanced features.

docs.title: 4.1 Mobile Application
docs.text:
• Touch targets: All interactive elements meet the minimum 44x44pt touch target size.
  • Colour contrast: Text meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).
  • Screen reader support: React Native Paper components provide built-in accessibility labels.
  • Keyboard navigation: Support for external keyboards on tablets.
  • Dynamic type: Text scales with system font size settings.
  • Dark mode: Full support for system dark/light mode preferences.

docs.title: 4.2 Web Application
docs.text:
• Keyboard navigation: All interactive elements are keyboard accessible.
  • Screen readers: Semantic HTML and ARIA labels are used where needed.
  • Colour contrast: All text meets WCAG AA standards.
  • Focus indicators: Clear focus states are provided for keyboard navigation.
  • Responsive design: Works on various screen sizes from mobile to desktop.
  • Alt text: All images include descriptive alt text.

docs.title: 5. Colour Palette
docs.text: All colours in the application are drawn from a forest-green and warm-cream palette. Custom hex values are used throughout. No Tailwind named colour shades (e.g., green-700) appear in the codebase.

docs.title: 5.1 Primary Brand Colours
docs.text: Token Hex Usage Forest Dark #254431 Page titles, section headings, strong text, header gradient start Forest Mid #356B43 Buttons, active states, icons, header gradient end, links Forest Light #86A98A Hover states, muted header text, secondary borders

docs.title: 5.2 Background Colours
docs.text: Token Hex Usage Warm Cream #F7F2EA Page background gradient from/to, preview panels, alternating rows Cool Sage #E4EBE4 Page background gradient via, borders, chips, hover fills

docs.title: 5.3 Text Colours
docs.text: Token Hex Usage Deep Charcoal #1E2520 Primary body text, high-contrast content Forest Dark #254431 Heading text, stat numbers, labels Muted Sage #7A8075 Captions, metadata, labels, placeholder text

docs.title: 5.4 Status and Semantic Colours
docs.text: Pair Usage #B91C1C / #FEE2E2 Error states, required badges, destructive actions #7F1D1D / #FEE2E2 Needs Review status badge (more than 730 days) #065F46 / #D1FAE5 Success, Recent inspection badge (fewer than 180 days) #92400E / #FEF3C7 Past Year badge (181–365 days) #9A3412 / #FFEDD5 Over 1 Year badge (366–730 days) #475569 / #F1F5F9 Never Inspected badge

docs.title: 5.5 Gradients
docs.text:
Header:     bg-gradient-to-r from-[#254431] to-[#356B43]
Background: bg-gradient-to-br from-[#F7F2EA] via-[#E4EBE4] to-[#F7F2EA]
Button:     bg-gradient-to-r from-[#356B43] to-[#254431]

docs.title: 6. Typography
docs.text: Typography is primarily handled through Tailwind utility classes, with global font setup in app/globals.css. The current web implementation loads Google Fonts Inter (body/UI) and DM Sans (headings), then applies them globally.

docs.title: 6.1 Type Scale
docs.text: Class Usage text-3xl font-bold Page titles (always white inside headers) text-2xl font-bold text-[#254431] Section headings within page body text-xl font-bold text-[#254431] Sub-section headings, card titles text-base / text-sm text-[#7A8075] Body text, subtitles in headers text-sm font-semibold text-[#7A8075] uppercase tracking-wide Label / caption above inputs and stat cards text-3xl font-bold text-[#254431] Stat numbers on cards text-xs font-semibold Badges, chips, type indicators text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-tight Micro-labels (Hidden badge, Live Draft pill)

docs.title: 6.2 Key Rules
docs.text:
• Never use text-4xl or larger inside the body area. Reserve large text for the header only.
  • All label text above inputs must be uppercase with tracking-wide.
  • Stat numbers always use text-3xl font-bold text-[#254431] regardless of which page they appear on.
  • Truncate long strings with the truncate class rather than wrapping.

docs.title: 7.1 Outer Page Shell
docs.text:
Most pages wrap their return in an outer div with the warm background gradient. This is the dominant pattern and should be used for new pages unless the page has a specific layout requirement.

<ProtectedRoute requireAdmin>
  <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-[#F7F2EA] via-[#E4EBE4] to-[#F7F2EA]">
    {/* Header */}
    <div className="bg-gradient-to-r from-[#254431] to-[#356B43] ...">
      ...
    </div>   {/* header closes HERE */}
    {/* Main Content */}
    <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6 py-6">
      ...
    </div>
  </div>
</ProtectedRoute>

docs.title: 7.2 Max Width Constraint
docs.text: max-w-7xl mx-auto is the standard container used across most pages (header interior, main content, alerts). Known exceptions exist (for example Form Editor header uses max-w-[100vw]) and should be treated as intentional per-page overrides.

docs.title: 7.3 Main Content Padding
docs.text: The main content div always uses px-6 py-6 for outer padding. Inner sections add their own vertical spacing via mb-6 or space-y-6.

docs.title: 7.4 Three-Column Admin Layout (Form Editor)
docs.text:
The Form Editor uses a special three-column layout inside the main content area:

  • Left sidebar: w-[220px] flex-shrink-0 - section navigation
  • Centre column: flex-1 min-w-0 - question list and editor
  • Right panel: w-[340px] flex-shrink-0 hidden lg:block - live preview (desktop only)

The three columns are wrapped in a flex gap-8 container inside a DndContext provider.

docs.title: 8. Header Component
docs.text: The application uses a shared green-header style but not one identical markup structure on every page. Most pages use a full-width dark-green banner with logo/title/subtitle and optional right-side actions.

docs.title: 8.1 Standard Header (Primary Site/Detail Pattern)
docs.text:
<div className="bg-gradient-to-r from-[#254431] to-[#356B43] text-white px-6 py-4 shadow-lg">
  <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto">
    {/* Optional back button */}
    <button className="flex items-center gap-1.5 text-[#86A98A] hover:text-white
                        transition-colors mb-4 group">
      <ArrowLeft className="w-4 h-4 group-hover:-translate-x-1 transition-transform" />
      <span className="text-sm font-medium">Back to Sites</span>
    </button>
    <div className="flex items-start justify-between">
      <div className="flex items-start gap-4">
        <Image src="/images/sapaa-icon-white.png" alt="SAPAA"
               width={140} height={140} priority
               className="h-16 w-auto flex-shrink-0 opacity-80 mt-1" />
        <div>
          <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold">{title}</h1>
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-[#E4EBE4] mt-1">
            <Icon className="w-5 h-5" />
            <span className="text-base">{subtitle}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
      {/* Right side: badge, status, or user pill */}
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

docs.title: 8.2 Admin Header (with AdminNavBar)
docs.text:
Admin pages include AdminNavBar near the top of the page. Some pages override nav background/shadow using the child selector approach:

<div className="[&>nav]:bg-none [&>nav]:bg-transparent [&>nav]:shadow-none
                [&>nav]:px-0 [&>nav]:py-0">
  <AdminNavBar />
</div>

docs.title: 8.3 Header with Action Button (Account Management)
docs.text:
When a page needs a primary action button in the header, it is grouped together with the AdminNavBar in a single right-side container:

<div className="flex items-center gap-3">
  <button onClick={...}
          className="bg-white text-[#356B43] px-6 py-3 rounded-xl font-semibold
                      hover:shadow-lg transition-all flex items-center gap-2">
    <UserPlus className="w-5 h-5" />
    Add User
  </button>
  <div className="[&>nav]:bg-none [&>nav]:bg-transparent ...">
    <AdminNavBar />
  </div>
</div>

docs.title: 8.4 SAPAA Logo
docs.text: Property Current Usage Source /images/sapaa-icon-white.png Size/CSS Varies by page (e.g., 140x140 with large header treatment, and compact 24x24 icon in report pages) Alt text Varies by page ("SAPAA" on many headers, "Logo" on current new/edit report pages) Priority Used where appropriate for above-the-fold logo rendering

docs.title: 8.5 Back Button
docs.text:
Back navigation exists on detail/report pages but currently has two variants: - Text + icon ("Back to Sites") on Site Detail. - Icon-only circular button (ArrowLeft) on New/Edit Report pages.

Property Current Usage Text Site Detail uses "Back to Sites"; New/Edit Report currently use icon-only back buttons Icon ArrowLeft used in all back-navigation variants Spacing/Placement Page-specific (either above header row as text link, or inline in title row as icon button)

docs.title: 9. Stats Cards
docs.text: Two stats card styles exist in the application. The Large Icon style is the preferred convention for all admin pages.

docs.title: 9.1 Large Icon Style (Preferred - Admin Pages)
docs.text:
Used on the Admin Dashboard, Account Management, and any new admin pages. Features a 48x48 gradient icon container beside the label and value:

<div className="bg-white rounded-2xl p-6 border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] shadow-sm">
  <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
    <div className="w-12 h-12 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#356B43] to-[#254431]
                    rounded-xl flex items-center justify-center">
      <Icon className="w-6 h-6 text-white" />
    </div>
    <div>
      <div className="text-sm font-semibold text-[#7A8075] uppercase tracking-wide">
        Label
      </div>
      <div className="text-3xl font-bold text-[#254431]">{value}</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

docs.title: 9.2 Compact Style (Legacy - Site List Header)
docs.text:
Used only in the HomeClient stats strip. Kept for backward compatibility but should not be used on new pages.

<div className="bg-white rounded-xl p-4 border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] shadow-sm">
  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-2">
    <Icon className="w-5 h-5 text-[#356B43]" />
    <div className="text-sm text-[#7A8075] font-medium uppercase tracking-wide">Label</div>
  </div>
  <div className="text-3xl font-bold text-[#254431]">{value}</div>
</div>

docs.title: 9.3 Stats Grid Layout
docs.text: Property Value Grid grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 Card radius rounded-2xl (large style) / rounded-xl (compact) Card padding p-6 (large) / p-4 (compact) Border border-2 border-[#E4EBE4]

docs.title: 9.4 Coloured Card Variants
docs.text:
When a card needs semantic colour (e.g., role summaries, status counts), override the background and border:

Variant Classes Green bg-[#D1FAE5] border-2 border-[#065F46]/20 Red bg-[#FEE2E2] border-2 border-[#B91C1C]/20 Amber bg-[#FEF3C7] border-2 border-[#92400E]/20

docs.title: 9.5 Clickable / Linked Card
docs.text:
When a card is a navigation link (e.g., Image Gallery on the dashboard), wrap it in a Next.js Link with block display and hover elevation:

<Link href="/admin/gallery" className="block">
  <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl p-6 border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] shadow-sm
                  hover:border-[#86A98A] hover:shadow-lg transition-all h-full">
    ...
  </div>
</Link>

docs.title: 10. Status Badges
docs.text: Inspection status is communicated through inline coloured badges. Badge style follows the pattern: rounded-full px-3 py-1 text-xs font-semibold.

docs.title: 10.1 Inspection Status Badges
docs.text: Label Text colour Background Condition Never Inspected #475569 #F1F5F9 No inspection on record Recently Inspected #065F46 #D1FAE5 Within 180 days Inspected This Year #92400E #FEF3C7 181–365 days Over 1 Year Ago #9A3412 #FFEDD5 366–730 days Needs Review #7F1D1D #FEE2E2 More than 730 days

docs.text: Badge Condition Required Shown on question cards when is_required = true Hidden Shown on question card when is_active = false - bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 LIVE DRAFT Shown on preview panel header when Add Question form is open - animate-pulse

docs.title: 10.3 General Badge Pattern
docs.text:
{/* Standard badge */}
<span className="text-xs font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full">
  {label}
</span>

{/* Micro badge (10px) for cards */}
<span className="text-[10px] px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full font-bold uppercase tracking-tight">

docs.title: 11.1 Primary Button
docs.text:
Used for the main action on a page or section. Always uses the forest gradient.

<button className="flex items-center gap-2 px-4 py-2.5
                   bg-gradient-to-r from-[#356B43] to-[#254431]
                   text-white text-sm font-semibold rounded-xl
                   hover:shadow-lg transition-all disabled:opacity-50">
  <Icon className="w-4 h-4" />
  Label
</button>

docs.title: 11.2 Secondary / Ghost Button
docs.text:
Used alongside a primary button for cancel or alternate actions.

<button className="px-4 py-2.5 border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] text-[#7A8075]
                   text-sm font-semibold rounded-xl hover:bg-[#E4EBE4]
                   transition-all">
  Cancel
</button>

docs.title: 11.3 Header Primary Button (White on Green)
docs.text:
Used when a primary action lives inside the green header. Background is white with green text to contrast against the dark header.

<button className="bg-white text-[#356B43] px-6 py-3 rounded-xl font-semibold
                   hover:shadow-lg transition-all flex items-center gap-2">
  <Icon className="w-5 h-5" />
  Add User
</button>

docs.title: 11.4 Icon-Only Buttons
docs.text:
Small icon buttons used inside cards and list rows:

Variant Classes Edit w-7 h-7 rounded-lg text-[#356B43] hover:bg-[#EEF5EF] Toggle p-1.5 rounded-md - active: text-[#7A8075] hover:bg-[#F7F2EA] / inactive: text-amber-600 bg-amber-50 hover:bg-amber-100 Delete w-8 h-8 rounded-lg text-[#B91C1C] hover:bg-[#FEE2E2] Close ml-auto on alert banners

docs.title: 11.5 Text Link Button
docs.text:
Used for secondary in-body actions like "+ Add a question":

<button className="text-sm text-[#356B43] font-semibold hover:underline
                   flex items-center gap-1">
  <Plus className="w-3.5 h-3.5" />
  Add a question
</button>

docs.title: 11.6 Section Add Button (Sidebar)
docs.text:
Small square icon button in a sidebar header:

<button className="w-7 h-7 bg-[#E4EBE4] hover:bg-[#356B43] hover:text-white
                   text-[#356B43] rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center
                   transition-all">
  <Plus className="w-4 h-4" />
</button>

docs.title: 12.1 Standard Text Input
docs.text:
<input type="text"
  className="w-full px-3 py-2.5 border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] rounded-xl text-sm
             focus:outline-none focus:border-[#356B43] transition-colors
             placeholder:text-[#7A8075]" />

docs.title: 12.2 Textarea
docs.text:
<textarea rows={2}
  className="w-full px-3 py-2.5 border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] rounded-xl text-sm
             focus:outline-none focus:border-[#356B43] resize-none transition-colors

<input type="checkbox"
  className="w-4 h-4 text-[#356B43] rounded focus:ring-[#356B43]" />

docs.title: 12.4 Label Pattern
docs.text:
All input labels follow the same uppercase tracking-wide pattern and sit above the input with mt-1 on the control:

<label className="text-xs font-semibold text-[#7A8075] uppercase tracking-wide">
  Field Name
</label>
<input className="... mt-1" />

docs.title: 12.5 Input Container (Inline Form Card)
docs.text:
When a mini-form appears inline (Add Section form, Edit Question form), it is wrapped in:

<div className="bg-white border-2 border-[#356B43] rounded-xl p-5 shadow-md">
  ...
</div>

The green border signals that this area is in an active edit state. The form actions bar at the bottom is separated by a top border:

<div className="flex gap-2 mt-5 pt-4 border-t-2 border-[#E4EBE4]">
  {/* primary + secondary buttons */}
</div>

docs.title: 13.1 Standard White Card
docs.text:
The default content container used throughout the application:

<div className="bg-white rounded-2xl border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] shadow-sm p-6">
  ...
</div>
Property Value Border radius rounded-2xl (preferred) or rounded-xl (compact contexts) Border border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] - always 2px, always sage green Shadow shadow-sm at rest, shadow-lg on hover (for interactive cards) Padding p-6 (standard) / p-4 (compact sidebar cards) / p-5 (form cards)

docs.title: 13.2 Sidebar Panel
docs.text:
<div className="bg-white rounded-2xl border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] p-4">
  <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
    <h3 className="text-sm font-bold text-[#254431] uppercase tracking-wide">
      Sections
    </h3>
    {/* action button */}
  </div>
  {/* list */}
</div>

docs.title: 13.3 Sticky Preview Panel
docs.text:
The right-side preview panel in the Form Editor is sticky so it stays visible while scrolling:

<div className="bg-[#F7F2EA] rounded-2xl border-2 border-[#E4EBE4] p-5 sticky top-6">
  ...
</div>

docs.title: 13.4 Active / Selected Card State
docs.text:
Cards that support selection toggle their border when selected:

{/* Default */}
border-[#E4EBE4] hover:border-[#86A98A]

{/* Selected */}
border-[#356B43] shadow-sm

docs.title: 13.5 Empty State Card
docs.text:
<div className="text-center py-16 bg-white rounded-2xl
                border-2 border-dashed border-[#E4EBE4]">
  <Icon className="w-12 h-12 text-[#E4EBE4] mx-auto mb-3" />
  <p className="text-[#7A8075] font-medium">No items yet.</p>
  <button className="mt-4 text-sm text-[#356B43] font-semibold hover:underline">
    + Add one
  </button>
